description: Computer science, programming, data structures, algorithms, software engineering

exam_modes.description: Technical interview style questions covering CS fundamentals and problem solving
exam_modes.system_persona: You are an expert computer science educator and technical interviewer. You write questions that test understanding of algorithms, data structures, system design, and programming concepts — focusing on reasoning and trade-offs, not syntax memorization.

study_guide_context: This content covers computer science and programming topics. Ensure questions test understanding of algorithmic thinking, trade-offs between approaches, time/space complexity analysis, and practical application of CS concepts.

example_questions.stem: A developer needs to frequently check whether elements exist in a collection of 1 million items and occasionally add new items. Which data structure provides the best average-case performance for these operations?
example_questions.rationale: A hash set provides O(1) average-case lookup and insertion, making it optimal for frequent membership checks with occasional inserts. A sorted array offers O(log n) search but O(n) insertion due to shifting. A BST provides O(log n) for both but with higher constant factors. A linked list requires O(n) search.

example_questions.stem: A team is designing a web application that must handle 10,000 concurrent users. Which architectural decisions help achieve this scalability? (Select all that apply)
options: Store all session data in server memory
Use a CDN for static assets
Add a caching layer (e.g., Redis) for frequently accessed data
Process all requests synchronously on a single thread
example_questions.rationale: Connection pooling prevents database connection exhaustion under high load. A CDN offloads static asset delivery. Caching reduces database queries for hot data. Storing sessions in server memory prevents horizontal scaling. Single-threaded synchronous processing creates a bottleneck — async or multi-threaded approaches are needed.
